reviews.review_msg: He did a great job explaining everything and his final was very reasonable

Great lectures with the slides posted ahead of time, readings can be a little dense (IMO you can just not read "The Ethical Life"), and he gives you the essay questions on the exam beforehand. In general he was just a pretty good prof.

Do not take this course if you don't have to. He does try to teach and is very passionate about his job, but it wasn't beneficial for the majority of the students. The first quiz went well, but it was all downhill from there. There were a tremendous amount of students that just walked out of the final.

Super unclear lectures. I seriously took nothing away from lectures and relied solely on the textbook because he would just ramble and use examples that made no sense. On top of that, attendance was mandatory. Good luck deciphering what Bruno is saying.

Guidon was not a good prof. He was an unorganized lecturer, most of his instructions were unclear and his lecture slides were riddled with mistakes. Guindon had high expectations for his students but did not set us up for success. His TA's were as confused as us if not more because they were not given clear instructions.

Super strict about attendance. Used tophat instead of canvas for the textbook, slides and assignments which was SUPER CONFUSING and I did not appreciate spending the extra money. Often got sidetracked by questions asked and would stare blankly and pause until he came up with an example. Genuinely cares that you understand and invites questions

Super respectful teacher who does understand where you are coming from. Lectures are mandatory as he doesn't share recordings unless a reason. 

Overall very nice and easier exams
Very good prof , good at what he does. He is just wants us to understand the topic and he does that very well. Attendance was mandatory and was graded as mark. would definitely take him again.

Bruno is outgoing and approachable, though many students found the grading to be tough. Grading scale was determined at the end of the semester, and as long as you do the practice problems/read the chapter (or show up to lecture) you'll do great on the exams. He also does a bunch of practice in class which helps in exams

Very passionate and genuine with his lessons and makes an effort to be lax and relatable to students. Used TopHat which makes you buy the textbook which isn't a huge deal but I was a little peeved. Explains things in a simple manner and frequently encourages questions. He wants you and your peers to succeed. If you interested in the topic, you good
If you take this class with this prof, I'd recommend that you pick a religion and start praying. TA's were also useless.

Solid prof. Lectures do a great job of breaking down the difficult readings. Midterm was fair as long as you studied the slides. Encourages questions from students. Marking seemed harsher than other profs, but that's only because he emphasizes precision in language. Overall, a really solid prof, but you'll have to work for your grade.

Bruno is one of the most helpful profs I have ever met. He is super passionate and knowledgable about the material. Yes, the topics can be a little complicated but he breaks them down super well and makes it really accessible. 10/10 would take again!

Amazing prof! Explains really clearly and gives super super helpful feedback on assignments. Grading and workload were reasonable, and bruno is really understanding, flexible, and caring. made the content really interesting!
Bruno Guindon is a logician who takes pride in his work. It is sad for me to say that his textbook and tests are not something he should be proud of. (Incredibly flawed)

loved bruno he was an amazing prof but the material itself was extremely hard to understand and needed lots of time outside of class to get an understanding of it. took this class hoping for an easy elective but turned out to be the hardest course of my semester, don't take unless ur prepared to spend a lot of time on the course

The prof isn't bad but the material is very hard. If you aren't good with memorizing different rules and all the diff ways you have to use them, don't take this class as a easy elective, also you need to spend 6-8 hours on this class on your own time and do the practice questions from the textbook. Overall, good prof but difficult class

Professor Guindon is a great guy and genuinely wants to see you succeed; however, the course material itself is difficult and requires a lot of time spent out of class to understand the content. tutorials are largely unhelpful, and the textbook did not help me understand the material. DO NOT take this class if you're looking for an easy elective.

Bruno is a good professor. He's knowledgeable and explains concepts well. However, it seemed his personal writing style would get in the way of being objective when grading student's papers. If it isn't the way he thinks a paper should be structured, he's rather inflexible. This is unlike other Philosophy profs. Wouldn't take again for this reason.
Bruno was a great prof who was passionate about logic. This made it more fun to learn the content even though I found it to be quite difficult. Tutorials definitely made all the difference.

Professor Guindon is very knowledgeable. This course was definitely tougher than other 200 level phil classes. There were weekly in-class group questions that had to be done within the hour, so that was pretty intense. Outside of that though, the papers are generally alright, and there are many paper topics provided.

Dr guidon is a really really great professor. He's very understanding and explains concepts really clearly. His lectures are very engaging. He has high standards for your work but as long as you put some effort in it's definitely very possible to do well. He's really helpful in office hours, so definitely recommend discussing assignments with him

Lkes talking a lot in lectures and encourages participation. for the class in general its pretty chill, i didn't do any readings and i still got a good grade. Bruno emphasizes that because this is a 100 level course, you aren't expected to know any philosophy writing styles so if you follow the same format for every paper you will do good.

Bruno was an amazing professor. He worked hard at making sure everyone understood what was going on and answering questions. He gave very clear explanations and instructions for any of the assignments. The mc final was ROUGH though.

Bruno was an amazing prof that was clearly passionate about the class. Reading could be pretty tough but he explained them fairly well. The papers were marked tough but my TA gave great feedback on them. Although I enjoyed the class it is definitely not the GPA booster I thought it would be.
